title: 10 Things to Check Before Taking Delivery of Your New Car
excerpt: A practical checklist covering the most important things every new car buyer should inspect before accepting delivery from the dealership.
content:
Buying a new car is a memorable experience, but excitement should never replace careful inspection. Before signing the delivery documents, spend some time checking the vehicle thoroughly. A few extra minutes can help you identify issues that are much easier for the dealership to resolve before delivery.

1. Inspect the Paint

Walk around the car in natural daylight and inspect every body panel. Look for scratches, paint mismatch, swirl marks, dents, or repainting.

2. Check Panel Gaps

Ensure the bonnet, doors, tailgate, and bumpers have even panel gaps. Uneven gaps may indicate poor assembly or previous repairs.

3. Verify All Glass

Inspect the windshield and all windows for chips, scratches, or cracks. Also verify that the manufacturing dates on the glass are reasonably close.

4. Test Every Light

Turn on the headlights, fog lamps, brake lights, indicators, reverse lamps, and cabin lights. Everything should operate correctly.

5. Examine the Tyres

Check tyre manufacturing dates, tyre pressure, tread condition, and ensure all four tyres are of the same brand and specification.

6. Inspect the Interior

Check the dashboard, seats, roof liner, steering wheel, carpets, and door trims for stains, scratches, or loose fittings.

7. Test Electronics

Verify the infotainment system, Bluetooth, Android Auto or Apple CarPlay, USB ports, wireless charging, reverse camera, parking sensors, and steering-mounted controls.

8. Review the Engine Bay

Open the bonnet and inspect fluid levels, battery terminals, wiring, and overall cleanliness. The engine bay should not show signs of leakage or damage.

9. Verify Accessories

Ensure you receive both keys, floor mats, toolkit, spare wheel, jack, owner's manual, warranty booklet, and any accessories promised by the dealership.

10. Check Documentation

Verify the VIN, engine number, insurance documents, invoice, temporary registration, and warranty details before taking delivery.

Final Thoughts

A careful inspection protects your investment and gives you confidence that your new vehicle is exactly as expected. If you're unsure what to inspect, an independent Pre-Delivery Inspection can provide complete peace of mind.
